title: euphrasia collina subsp. trichocalycina
intro: Euphrasia collina subsp. trichocalycina is a subspecies of the Euphrasia collina plant, commonly known as a type of eyebright, which is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating eye infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of Euphrasia collina subsp. trichocalycina include its ability to soothe and calm the eyes, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
